subject: Re: stripey stripe stripe
body:
To update the group.
I was able to resolve this by "downgrading" the mrc headers so that they 
are interpretable by Warp. Hopefully Dimitry will have a fix for this.

On Saturday, January 9, 2021 at 3:01:10 PM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:

> Hi Alister,
>
> Thanks for checking in. It doesnt appear that I am motion correcting with 
> too fine spatial resolution.I crossed-checked all the values and they are 
> in accordance with the header information in the files.
>
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
>
>
> On Sat, Jan 9, 2021 at 1:51 PM Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Pranav,
>>
>> I’ve noticed when reconstructing tomograms the images wrap around in the 
>> way you described if reconstructing an area larger than the images - Is it 
>> possible that you’re motion correcting with too fine spatial resolution and 
>> ending up with large shifts, causing this wrap around to fill the empty 
>> space?
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On 9 Jan 2021, at 12:41, Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>> Hi Dimitry,
>> S
>> o I took a look a closer look at the images and it appears that during 
>> motion correction in warp, the image is wrongly spliced and the stripe at 
>> the end of the image should actually be in the beginning. I ran motion 
>> correction with 1x1x8 grid and defocus with 2x2x1 grid so I dont think that 
>> should be the cause of the problem. In parallel I also processed a dataset 
>> collected on a K2 with similar preproc setttings and I dont see this 
>> transposition/splicing so it seems to be a falcon4 mrc file thing. I hope 
>> this information somehow helps in diagnosing the problem.
>>
>> Best,
>> Pranav
>> On Tuesday, January 5, 2021 at 8:51:49 PM UTC Pranav Shah wrote:
>>
>>> Apologies for the spam, it seems I cannot share data more than 8mb on 
>>> the forum, a decimated version of the image is now attached
>>> Best,
>>> Pranav
>>> --
>>> Pranav Shah
>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>
>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>> University of Oxford,
>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>> UK
>>>
>>>
>>> On Tue, Jan 5, 2021 at 8:13 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Bugger, the image failed to attach... try this one...
>>>> Best,
>>>> Pranav
>>>> --
>>>> Pranav Shah
>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>>>>
>>>> Division of Structural Biology,
>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
>>>> University of Oxford,
>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
>>>> UK
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Tue, Jan 5, 2021 at 8:12 PM Pranav Shah <p.sha...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>> Hi Dimitry,
>>>>>
>>>>> I am processing a Falcon 4 dataset and i am consistently seeing a band 
>>>>> in the right third of image following motion correction. I dont see this 
>>>>> artefact when I processed the same dataset using MotionCor2, could you help 
>>>>> understand what is causing this and if should fret about it? Image attached 
>>>>> for reference.
>>>>>
>>>>>
>>>>> Best,
>>>>> Pranav
>>>>>
